new_classes.label_de: Weinessig in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von mehr als 2 Litern (Unterposition 2209 00 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine vinegar obtained by acetous (acetic) fermentation of wine, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, as covered by CN subheading 2209 00 19 and Additional Note 11 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The key distinguishing criteria for CN 2209 00 19 versus its sibling 2209 00 11 are: (1) the product is wine vinegar (i.e. produced by acetous fermentation of wine), and (2) the container volume is > 2 litres. Both criteria are captured via the subClassOf hierarchy (WineVinegar2209), the producedBy restriction to WineAceticFermentationProcess, and the hasPart-someValuesFrom-LargeContainer restriction (complement of SmallContainer). Coverage is reduced to 0.75 because the legal note refers to Additional Note 11 to Chapter 22 (minimum acidity/composition requirements for wine vinegar) and HS Explanatory Notes Part I, second paragraph, point 1 – these compositional/chemical criteria (e.g. minimum volatile acid content) cannot be fully expressed with the current TBox quality vocabulary, as no AcidityQuality or VolatileAcidContent class exists. These chemical criteria are therefore not axiomatised.
